description:
['Durags (also spelled, "doo rag" or "do rag") can be worn as fashion statements, or simply as ways to keep hair tidy and looking fresh. Tying a durag is quick and easy. There are many techniques, and everyone has their personal favorite way.'
 'How to tie :'
 '1st. Place the durag on your head. Doo rags come in many types of fabrics. We provide Velvet and Silky Metallic. Line the center seams up with the center of your head for symmetry.keep the front edge sits between your eyebrows and your hairline.  2nd. Wrap the ties behind the head. Take one tie in each hand. Pull both ties back behind the head so that they cross in an “X” behind the head. Pull the ties back on their respective sides of the head. Ties should rest between the ear and the head so that the ears stay exposed. 3rd. Cross the ties in front of the head. Once you’ve crossed the ties in the back of your head, pull them back in front. Make sure they cross in the center of your forehead. Then pull them all the way to the back of your head. 4th. tie a knot. Tie the straps into a knot at the back of your head. Don’t knot it too tight in case you can’t loosen it later. Don’t pull too hard or it will feel uncomfortable. If you don’t want the ties to roll up like ropes, you can press them flat against your head as you pull them and wrap them. Warnings Durags are generally considered a part of African American culture. Anyone can wear one, of course, but those of other ethnic backgrounds should be aware of concerns regarding cultural sensitivity and cultural appropriation.']
